title: Hacker Speak: Essential Terms 🗣️
description: Speak like a pro: Exploit, Payload, and Zero-Day.

slides.title: The Glossary 📚
slides.content:
### Hackers have their own language.

Master these terms to understand more advanced topics later:

*   **Vulnerability**: A weakness or flaw (the 'open window').
*   **Exploit**: The tool or method used to take advantage of the weakness (the 'ladder to climb in').
*   **Payload**: The cargo: what the hacker does once inside (stealing files, installing a virus).

quizData.question: If a hacker uses a script to steal your credit card details after entering the system, what is that script called?
quizData.explanation: The payload is the actual malicious action or data being delivered to the target.

slides.title: The Zero-Day ⚡
slides.content:
### The Holy Grail of Hacking

A **Zero-Day Vulnerability** is a bug that is unknown to the software creator.

*   **Why 'Zero-Day'?**: Because the creator has had **zero days** to fix it.
*   These are incredibly valuable and dangerous!

slides.title: Terminology Map 🗺️
slides.content:
### Don't mix them up!

| Term | Real World Analogy |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Vulnerability** | An unlocked back door |
| **Exploit** | Turning the handle |
| **Payload** | Stealing the TV |
| **Patch** | Fixing the lock |
